frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current representations attracted toward previously encoded items

frameworks.name: working memory reconstruction via multivariate decoding
frameworks.one_line: MEG-based reconstruction of held motion directions across the trial

frameworks.name: neural reactivation of prior trial content
frameworks.one_line: past trial information re-emerges to bias current representation

core_question: Does a direct neural correlate of the behavioral attractive serial-dependence bias exist in working memory?
key_assumptions: behavioral attractive bias indexes serial dependence
MEG multivariate signals reconstruct held motion direction reliably
retro-cue isolates encoding from selection for report
manipulations: two sequentially presented motion directions per trial
retro-cue selecting one of the two memoranda for later report
across-trial relation between previous target and current item
key_findings: multivariate reconstructions recovered both memorized motion directions
reconstructed current direction was attractively shifted toward previous trial's target
neural attractive bias mirrored the behavioral attractive bias
neural bias emerged at post-encoding rather than encoding time points
interpretations: reconstructed shifts constitute a direct neural signature of serial dependence
the bias arises during post-encoding working-memory stages, not initial sensory encoding
